---
title: "bigwig_analysis"
author: "Daniel Arango"
date: "2/24/2026"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r bigwig, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, fig.width=4, fig.height=3, fig.path='R/', dev=c('png', 'pdf')}

# ===============================
# Load libraries
# ===============================
library(rtracklayer)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# ===============================
# Detect all BigWig files
# ===============================
bw_files <- c("SN1.bw", "SN2.bw", "SN4.bw", "SN5.bw", "SN6.bw", "SN7.bw", "SN9.bw", "SN10.bw", "SL1.bw", "SL2.bw", "SL4.bw", "SL5.bw", "SL6.bw", "SL7.bw", "SL9.bw", "SL10.bw", "SL11.bw", "SL12.bw", "SL14.bw", "SL15.bw", "SL16.bw", "SL17.bw", "SL19.bw", "SL20.bw" )

# ===============================
# Define genomic region
# ===============================
chr <- "GL000220.1" # Specify the chromosome 
start <- 109078 # Start position 
end <- 110946 # End position

region <- GRanges(seqnames = chr, ranges = IRanges(start = start, end = end))

# ===============================
# Safe BigWig extraction function
# ===============================
# Initialize a data frame to store results 
positions <- seq(start, end)
results <- data.frame(Position = positions) 

# Loop through each sample 
for (file in bw_files) { 
  sample_name <- basename(file) # Extract sample name from file path 
bw_data <- import(file, which = region) 

# Extract RPKM values 
rpkm_values <- as.data.frame(bw_data)[, "score"] # Adjust "score" if needed 

# Ensure rpkm_values is of the same length as positions 
if (length(rpkm_values) < length(positions)) { 
  
  # Fill with 0 for missing positions 
  rpkm_values <- c(rpkm_values, rep(0, length(positions) - length(rpkm_values))) 
  } 

# Add RPKM values to the results data frame 
results[[sample_name]] <- rpkm_values[1:length(positions)] # Align length 
}


# ===============================
# Define subtraction pairs
# ===============================
pairs <- tribble(
  ~new_name,       ~clip,       ~input,
  "parental_1",    "SN6.bw",    "SN1.bw",
  "parental_2",    "SL6.bw",    "SL1.bw",
  "parental_3",    "SL16.bw",   "SL11.bw",
  "pDA98_1",       "SN7.bw",    "SN2.bw",
  "pDA98_2",       "SL7.bw",    "SL2.bw",
  "pDA98_3",       "SL17.bw",   "SL12.bw",
  "pDA154_1",      "SN9.bw",    "SN4.bw",
  "pDA154_2",      "SL9.bw",    "SL4.bw",
  "pDA154_3",      "SL19.bw",   "SL14.bw",
  "pDA156_1",      "SN10.bw",   "SN5.bw",
  "pDA156_2",      "SL10.bw",   "SL5.bw",
  "pDA156_3",      "SL20.bw",   "SL15.bw"
)


#Reference
#pDA98 = NAT10-WT
#pDA154 = E389L
#pDA156 = K290A

# ===============================
# Perform safe subtraction
# ===============================
InputSubtracted <- data.frame(
  Position = positions - start + 1
)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(pairs))) {
  
  clip_name  <- pairs$clip[i]
  input_name <- pairs$input[i]
  new_name   <- pairs$new_name[i]
  
  if (clip_name %in% names(results) &&
      input_name %in% names(results)) {
    
    InputSubtracted[[new_name]] <-
      results[[clip_name]] - results[[input_name]]
    
  } else {
    
    warning("Skipping subtraction for: ", new_name,
            " (missing file column)")
  }
}

InputSubtracted[, -1] <- lapply(
  InputSubtracted[, -1],
  function(x) x / max(x, na.rm = TRUE)
)

```


```{r plots, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=2, fig.path='plots/', dev=c('png', 'pdf')}

# ===============================
# Libraries
# ===============================
library(ggplot2)
library(zoo)

# ===============================
# Parameters
# ===============================
window_size <- 20   # smoothing window
y_max <- 1          # fixed y-axis limit

# ===============================
# Define groups
# ===============================
groups <- list(
  pDA98 = grep("^pDA98_", names(InputSubtracted), value = TRUE),
  pDA154 = grep("^pDA154_", names(InputSubtracted), value = TRUE),
  pDA156 = grep("^pDA156_", names(InputSubtracted), value = TRUE),
  parental = grep("^parental_", names(InputSubtracted), value = TRUE)
)

# ===============================
# Smooth replicates, compute mean + SEM
# ===============================
plot_data_list <- lapply(names(groups), function(g) {
  cols <- groups[[g]]
  
  # Smooth each replicate first
  smoothed_reps <- apply(InputSubtracted[, cols], 2, function(x) {
    zoo::rollmean(x, k = window_size, fill = NA, align = "center")
  })
  
  # Compute mean and SEM
  mean_signal <- rowMeans(smoothed_reps, na.rm = TRUE)
  sem_signal  <- apply(smoothed_reps, 1, function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)/sqrt(length(x)))
  
  # Clip negatives
  mean_signal <- pmax(mean_signal, 0)
  sem_signal  <- pmax(sem_signal, 0)
  
  data.frame(
    Position = InputSubtracted$Position,
    mean_signal = mean_signal,
    sem_signal  = sem_signal,
    group = g
  )
})

plot_data_long <- do.call(rbind, plot_data_list)

# ===============================
# Plot with ribbons
# ===============================
for(g in unique(plot_data_long$group)) {
  df <- subset(plot_data_long, group == g)
  
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Position, y = mean_signal)) +
    
    # Area under the curve
    geom_area(fill = "blue", alpha = 0.4) +
    
    # Line on top
    geom_line(color = "blue", size = 1) +
    
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, y_max)) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      expand = c(0,0),
      breaks = seq(0, max(plot_data_long$Position), by = 200)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0, y_max)) +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(
      y = "Mean Signal",
      title = paste0(g, " (smoothed)")
    )
  
  print(p)
}

```


```{r colored, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=2, fig.path='plots/', dev=c('png', 'pdf')}

# ===============================
# Libraries
# ===============================
library(ggplot2)
library(zoo)

# ===============================
# Parameters
# ===============================
window_size <- 20   # smoothing window
y_max <- 1          # fixed y-axis limit

# ===============================
# Define groups
# ===============================
groups <- list(
  pDA98 = grep("^pDA98_", names(InputSubtracted), value = TRUE),
  pDA154 = grep("^pDA154_", names(InputSubtracted), value = TRUE),
  pDA156 = grep("^pDA156_", names(InputSubtracted), value = TRUE),
  parental = grep("^parental_", names(InputSubtracted), value = TRUE)
)

# ===============================
# Smooth replicates, compute mean + SEM
# ===============================
plot_data_list <- lapply(names(groups), function(g) {
  cols <- groups[[g]]
  
  # Smooth each replicate first
  smoothed_reps <- apply(InputSubtracted[, cols], 2, function(x) {
    zoo::rollmean(x, k = window_size, fill = NA, align = "center")
  })
  
  # Compute mean and SEM
  mean_signal <- rowMeans(smoothed_reps, na.rm = TRUE)
  sem_signal  <- apply(smoothed_reps, 1, function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)/sqrt(length(x)))
  
  # Clip negatives
  mean_signal <- pmax(mean_signal, 0)
  sem_signal  <- pmax(sem_signal, 0)
  
  data.frame(
    Position = InputSubtracted$Position,
    mean_signal = mean_signal,
    sem_signal  = sem_signal,
    group = g
  )
})

plot_data_long <- do.call(rbind, plot_data_list)

# ===============================
# Plot with ribbons and colored regions
# ===============================
for(g in unique(plot_data_long$group)) {
  df <- subset(plot_data_long, group == g)
  
  # Define region colors
  df$region <- with(df,
    ifelse(Position >= 1000 & Position <= 1300, "blue",
    ifelse(Position >= 1600, "cyan", "gray"))
  )
  
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Position, y = mean_signal)) +
    
    # Area under the curve colored by region
    geom_area(aes(fill = region), alpha = 0.4) +
    
    # Line on top
    geom_line(color = "black", size = 1) +
    
    # Define manual colors
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        "blue" = "blue",
        "cyan" = "cyan",
        "gray" = "gray"
      )
    ) +
    
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, y_max)) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      expand = c(0,0),
      breaks = seq(0, max(plot_data_long$Position), by = 200)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0, y_max)) +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(
      y = "Mean Signal",
      title = paste0(g, " (smoothed)")
    )
  
  print(p)
}

```
